title: Minimum Attendance Requirement
body: Students must maintain a minimum of 75% attendance as per RTU regulations. Falling short will debar them from appearing in Mid-Term exams.
question: What is the minimum attendance required?
answer: Students must maintain a minimum of 75% attendance as per RTU regulations. Falling short will debar them from appearing in Mid-Term exams.

title: Low Attendance Consequences
body: If your attendance falls below 75%, you will not be allowed to appear for the Mid-Term examinations.
question: What happens if my attendance is below 75%?
answer: You will not be allowed to appear for the Mid-Term examinations.

title: Mid-Term Exam Attendance Policy
body: It is compulsory to appear in both Mid-Term exams. Failing to appear in both will result in being marked absent in internal marks and may cause an internal backlog.
question: Is it mandatory to appear in both Mid-Term exams?
answer: Yes, it is compulsory. Failing to appear in both will result in being marked absent in internal marks and may cause an internal backlog.

title: Single Mid-Term Exam Appearance
body: If you appear in only one Mid-Term exam, you will be awarded average marks. However, if you fail due to these marks, the responsibility lies solely with you.
question: What if I appear in only one Mid-Term exam?
answer: You will be awarded average marks. However, if you fail due to these marks, the responsibility lies solely with you.

title: Unfair Means in Exams
body: Using unfair means in exams will result in zero marks in that subject, and your parents will be informed immediately.
question: What are the consequences of using unfair means in exams?
answer: You will receive zero marks in that subject, and your parents will be informed immediately.

title: Improvement Exam Eligibility
body: Only students with valid medical reasons or those who appeared in both Mid-Terms but scored poorly are eligible for improvement exams.
question: Who is eligible for improvement exams?
answer: Only students with valid medical reasons or those who appeared in both Mid-Terms but scored poorly are eligible.

title: Assignment Importance
body: Assignments are important as their marks are included in internal assessment, but they alone do not guarantee good marks. Consistent performance is required.
question: Are assignments important?
answer: Yes. Assignment marks are included in internal assessment, but they alone do not guarantee good marks. Consistent performance is required.

title: Late Assignment Submission
body: Late submissions may lead to loss of marks since timely submission is mandatory.
question: What happens if I don't submit assignments on time?
answer: Late submissions may lead to loss of marks since timely submission is mandatory.

title: College Fee Payment Rules
body: All fees must be paid on time. Students with pending dues may be debarred from academic activities.
question: What are the rules regarding college fees?
answer: All fees must be paid on time. Students with pending dues may be debarred from academic activities.

title: Ragging and Faculty Misbehavior
body: Ragging or misbehavior with faculty results in immediate rustication. Parents must visit the college to appeal against the decision.
question: What is the punishment for ragging or misbehavior with faculty?
answer: Immediate rustication. Parents must visit the college to appeal against the decision.

title: Mobile Phone Usage During Class
body: Students cannot use mobile phones during class hours. Those found watching movies or gaming on phones during class will be debarred, and parents will be called.
question: Can I use my mobile phone during class hours?
answer: No. Students found watching movies or gaming on phones during class will be debarred, and parents will be called.

title: Mobile Phone Ban Policy
body: Mobile phones are not completely banned, but misuse during class hours is strictly prohibited.
question: Are mobile phones completely banned?
answer: Not entirely, but misuse during class hours is strictly prohibited.

title: Vehicle Safety Rules
body: Wearing a helmet is mandatory for all students commuting on their own vehicles.
question: What safety rule is there for students using vehicles?
answer: Wearing a helmet is mandatory for all students commuting on their own vehicles.

title: Helmet Violation Consequences
body: Not wearing a helmet while commuting will be considered a violation of college safety rules and may face disciplinary action.
question: What happens if I don't wear a helmet while commuting?
answer: You will be considered in violation of college safety rules and may face disciplinary action.

title: Code of Conduct Importance
body: The Code of Conduct is important because it ensures discipline, academic integrity, safety, and a healthy learning environment for all students.
question: Why is the Code of Conduct important?
answer: It ensures discipline, academic integrity, safety, and a healthy learning environment for all students.
